content: makefile-hierarchical-help: Makefile with categorized help output (Getting Started, Development, Testing, Quality Gates, Database, AWS LocalStack, Utilities) improves discoverability. Default target shows full help menu with 25+ commands organized by purpose.

env-example-bun-hot-reload: Bun's --watch flag (packages/chat-gateway: bun run --watch src/server.ts) provides zero-config TypeScript hot reload for local development. No nodemon/tsx/ts-node-dev needed.

name: seed-data-json-fixtures
description: JSON fixture files (tenants.json, skills.json) + TypeScript seeder script with @aws-sdk/client-dynamodb provides type-safe test data initialization. Seeder supports --create-tables, --seed-data, --delete-tables flags for flexible workflows.

content: monorepo-cli-integration: CLI packages must be added to root package.json scripts: build:cli target, test:cli target, and lint:packages command chain. Workspace dependency references use 'workspace:*' for @chimera/shared and @chimera/core.

name: cli-package-structure
description: CLI packages in Chimera monorepo use Commander.js for command parsing, chalk for colored output, ora for spinners, inquirer for prompts, and table for tabular output. Package structure: bin/chimera (executable wrapper) → dist/cli.js (compiled from src/cli.ts). Command modules in src/commands/ register subcommands via register*Commands(program) pattern. Config stored in ~/.chimera/config.json via utils/config.ts.

content: aws-sdk-peerdependencies: AWS SDK v3 clients defined as peerDependencies in packages/core/package.json. Discovery module adds @aws-sdk/client-cost-explorer, @aws-sdk/client-resource-groups-tagging-api, @aws-sdk/client-config. Pattern: Dynamic imports (await import('@aws-sdk/...')) within methods to avoid requiring deps at module load time.

name: activity-logging-decision-capture
description: Activity logging module captures agent decision-making with DecisionLog type (question, alternatives, selected option, justification, confidence), WellArchitectedEvaluation mapping decisions to 6 AWS pillars (Operational Excellence, Security, Reliability, Performance Efficiency, Cost Optimization, Sustainability) with 0-10 scores, DecisionLogger service class with calculateConfidence() (score gap + diversity), determineRiskLevel() (low/medium/high/critical), queryDecisions() filtering, and generateAnalytics() aggregating patterns. Pattern: preserve agent mental model (all alternatives not just winner), multi-criteria decision analysis with pillar weights, cost transparency (immediate/monthly/per-transaction), DynamoDB storage with PK=TENANT#{id} SK=ACTIVITY#{timestamp}#{activityId}, TTL for retention. Enables post-hoc why-did-agent-choose-X queries and continuous learning from decision patterns.

content: Standard DynamoDB service class structure: (1) DynamoDBClient interface for dependency injection, (2) Config interface with tableName and dynamodb client, (3) Service class with CRUD methods, (4) Helper methods for PK/SK formatting (e.g., TENANT#{id}, ACTION#{timestamp}#{id}), (5) TTL calculation methods (Unix timestamp), (6) Error handling for ConditionalCheckFailedException on put operations, (7) Query methods with KeyConditionExpression and optional FilterExpression, (8) GSI queries with IndexName parameter

name: activity-dashboard-aggregation
description: StatusDashboard class aggregates real-time agent activity from sessions table: (1) session metrics (active/idle/terminated counts, total messages, token usage), (2) agent-level summaries with per-agent metrics, (3) pagination with base64-encoded tokens, (4) tenant-wide dashboard combining all metrics. Follows CostTracker pattern: DynamoDB client interface, config object, public query methods, private aggregation helpers.

content: AWS SDK peerDependencies for infra-builder: @aws-sdk/client-codecommit, @aws-sdk/client-codepipeline, @aws-sdk/client-cloudformation added to packages/core/package.json peerDependencies following established pattern to avoid version conflicts when consuming @chimera/core.
AWS SDK v3 clients added as peerDependencies in packages/core/package.json. Package name correction: @aws-sdk/client-config (incorrect) -> @aws-sdk/client-config-service (correct)

name: mutable-builder-readonly-return
description: When building objects with readonly properties incrementally, use mutable temporary type during construction, then return as readonly type. Example: type MutableAccountCost without readonly, build with mutations, return as AccountCostBreakdown with readonly. TypeScript structural typing makes mutable type assignable to readonly type.

content: well-architected-sdk-peerdependency: @aws-sdk/client-wellarchitected added to packages/core/package.json peerDependencies following established pattern for AWS SDK v3 clients. All AWS SDK clients are peer dependencies to avoid version conflicts across monorepo.

aws-sdk-dependencies-pattern: AWS SDK v3 clients must be peerDependencies only, never regular dependencies. This prevents version conflicts and allows consuming packages to control SDK versions. Move all @aws-sdk/client-* packages from dependencies to peerDependencies section.

name: barrel-export-conflict-resolution
description: Wildcard exports (export *) in barrel files cause namespace collisions when multiple modules define types with the same name. Solution: Replace with explicit exports and rename conflicting types using 'as' keyword. Common conflicts: RiskLevel (activity vs well-architected have different semantics), ISOTimestamp (multiple modules define string), StageStatus (swarm refinement vs pipeline execution), AgentRole (orchestration coordination vs swarm problem-solving).

name: dynamodb-query-params-typing
description: Replace 'params: any' with explicit TypeScript types for DynamoDB query/scan parameters. Define interface with TableName, IndexName?, KeyConditionExpression?, FilterExpression?, ExpressionAttributeValues, ExpressionAttributeNames?, Limit? fields to catch type errors at compile time.

name: aws-sdk-v3-type-mapping
description: AWS SDK v3 enum/type conversions: CostMetric (UnblendedCost → UNBLENDED_COST), FileModeTypeEnum (NORMAL/EXECUTABLE/SYMLINK only), GroupDefinition (Type/Key capitalized). SDK response properties often missing from types, require 'as any' cast.

name: eslint-flat-config-compatibility
description: ESLint 10 uses flat config (eslint.config.js), ESLint 8 uses .eslintrc.json. When packages have mixed versions, use ESLint 8 with .eslintrc.json for compatibility. Remove --ext flag from lint scripts for flat config support.

name: typescript-incremental-build-staleness
description: TypeScript tsbuildinfo can become stale in git worktrees, causing false 'file not built from source' errors. Solution: rm tsconfig.tsbuildinfo before rebuilding packages/shared.

name: bun-test-path-scoping
description: Use './directory/' prefix for path-based test scoping. 'bun test packages/ infra/' treats arguments as filename filters and still searches all workspaces. Must use 'bun test ./packages/ ./infra/' to properly scope tests to specific directories.

content: Root package.json test script must scope to 'bun test ./packages/ ./infra/' to exclude integration/e2e tests that require a running server. Integration tests available via separate scripts (test:integration, test:e2e).

AWS SDK tool files use retryWithBackoff<T> generic method. TypeScript cannot infer T through overloaded SDK send() methods. All retryWithBackoff calls must specify explicit CommandOutput type parameters (e.g., retryWithBackoff<PutObjectCommandOutput>).

name: strands-sdk-type-shim
description: When importing from unpublished npm packages (@strands-agents/sdk), create ambient module declaration (.d.ts) files to provide TypeScript types. Pattern: (1) Create packages/core/src/<package>-shim.d.ts with 'declare module' block, (2) Define exported interfaces and functions matching expected API, (3) Force-add to git with 'git add -f' to override *.d.ts gitignore rule. Allows TypeScript compilation without actual package installed.

name: strands-tool-factory-pattern
description: AWS tool conversion from class-based OOP to functional Strands tools. Pattern: (1) Factory functions capture clientFactory in closure, (2) Each tool uses tool() decorator with Zod input schema + async callback, (3) Flatten context params (tenantId/agentId/region) directly into Zod schema instead of separate AWSToolContext object, (4) Return JSON.stringify for all results (success + error), (5) Binary data handled via base64 encoding/decoding (S3 body, Lambda zipFile). Tools become discrete LLM choices rather than methods on class instance. 24 tools across 4 AWS services.

content: strands-ts-sdk-not-published: @strands-agents/sdk (npm) is at v0.0.1-development and NOT published to npm yet (as of 2026-03-21). Use declare module shim for TypeScript type-checking. Package uses zod v4 (^4.1.12) but zod v3 is compatible for schema definitions. Import: import { tool } from '@strands-agents/sdk'. Tool format: tool({ name, description, inputSchema: z.object({...}), callback: async (input) => string }).

name: in-memory-test-client
description: Test implementations use Map-based storage for fast, dependency-free testing. InMemoryClient implements MemoryClient interface with Map<string, MemoryEntry> for entries and Map<string, SessionState> for sessions. Strategy selection uses simple heuristics (content.includes checks). Pattern: constructor(namespace), async methods, clear separation from production clients via factory.

name: namespace-parsing-non-greedy-regex
description: Namespace parsing with IDs containing hyphens requires non-greedy regex. Pattern /^tenant-(.+?)-user-(.+)$/ uses .+? (non-greedy) instead of .+ to correctly extract 'tenant-123' from 'tenant-tenant-123-user-user-456'. Greedy .+ would capture 'tenant-123-user' as tenantId. Critical for multi-tenant isolation - parsing errors could leak data.

name: memory-client-tier-config-pattern
description: TypeScript memory integration mirrors Python agent pattern — InMemoryClient for testing, tier-based config (Basic STM=10, Advanced STM=50, Premium STM=200), strategy selection (SUMMARY all, USER_PREFERENCE advanced+, SEMANTIC premium), auto message storage in ChimeraAgent.invoke(). MemoryClientFactory uses lazy require() to avoid circular imports.

content: Memory namespace parseNamespace() regex must use non-greedy (.+?) for tenantId to handle hyphenated IDs correctly. Greedy (.+) would consume hyphens meant for the -user- separator boundary. E.g. tenant-my-tenant-user-bob: greedy captures my-tenant-user-bob as tenantId, non-greedy captures my-tenant.

name: react-loop-bedrock-integration
description: ReAct loop implementation for LLM agents with Bedrock Converse API. Pattern: (1) Build messages array with user message, (2) Loop max 10 iterations calling model.converse(), (3) Check stopReason - if 'end_turn' extract text and break, if 'tool_use' find toolUse blocks in response.output.message.content, (4) Execute matched tools via callback(input), (5) Add assistant message + tool_result messages to conversation, (6) Continue loop. Tool specs converted from Zod schema to JSON Schema for Bedrock API. Backward compatible - returns placeholder when no model provided.

name: mock-model-test-pattern
description: MockModel test pattern for deterministic agent testing. MockModel accepts array of MockResponse (text, toolCalls, stopReason) and simulates Bedrock Converse API. Test structure: (1) Create mock tools with Zod schemas and callbacks, (2) Configure MockModel with response sequence (toolCallResponse → textResponse for 2-turn ReAct), (3) Pass model + loadedTools to agent config, (4) Assert on result.output, result.toolCalls, and model.getCallCount(). Acceptance test validates full pipeline: user request → tool execution → final response. InMemoryClient.retrieve({limit: N}) used to verify memory storage instead of getRecentMessages().

content: react-loop-pattern-ts: ChimeraAgent.invoke() implements ReAct loop when model is injected via AgentConfig.model. Loop: build messages → model.converse() → check tool_use → execute tool.callback() → feed toolResult back → repeat until end_turn or max_iterations(10). Backward compatible: returns placeholder when no model. MockModel from tests/helpers/mock-model.ts satisfies the model interface for deterministic testing.

name: bedrock-model-adapter-pattern
description: BedrockModel class implements same interface as MockModel (converse() returning ConverseResponse with output.message.content[]) for agent integration. Converts between ChimeraAgent ContentBlock format and AWS Bedrock native format. Added as peerDependency @aws-sdk/client-bedrock-runtime^3.0.0.

content: chat-gateway-config-pattern: Chat gateway uses singleton config loaded from environment (BEDROCK_MODEL_ID, BEDROCK_REGION, BEDROCK_ENABLED, etc). BedrockModel created conditionally based on config.bedrock.enabled flag, falling back to placeholder responses when disabled.

name: bun-monorepo-docker-build
description: Multi-stage Dockerfile for Bun monorepos: (1) builder stage installs all workspace deps and builds packages in dependency order, (2) runtime stage copies only production deps and dist artifacts, (3) runs as non-root user (chimera:1001), (4) includes wget-based health check on /health endpoint.

name: bedrock-model-adapter-pattern
description: BedrockModel class wraps AWS Bedrock Converse API with bidirectional content block conversion. Config-driven feature toggle via BEDROCK_ENABLED env var.

name: skill-md-parser-bridge
description: SKILL.md v2 parser uses js-yaml for robust frontmatter parsing (YAML + markdown body). Skill bridge loads skills from registry, converts to agent tools (instruction → prompt injection, MCP → callable tools). SystemPromptTemplate requires class instantiation (new SystemPromptTemplate(template)). SkillRegistry.getInstalledSkills() returns SkillInstall[], not string[]. Tests cover parsing (permissions, dependencies, MCP config, tests) and agent integration (tool conversion, prompt enhancement, catalog building).

name: glob-to-regex-conversion
description: Safe glob-to-regex conversion pattern: (1) Replace glob wildcards with temporary placeholders (** → <!DOUBLESTAR!>, * → <!STAR!>, ? → <!QUESTION!>), (2) Escape special regex characters [.+^()|[\]\\], (3) Replace placeholders with regex equivalents (** → .*, * → [^/]*, ? → [^/]). This prevents regex syntax errors from wildcards being escaped before conversion. Used for filesystem permission pattern matching in SkillTrustEngine.matchGlob().

content: DynamoDB client interface must include update() method for UpdateExpression queries. Using put() for UpdateExpression operations (like incrementing counters) is incorrect - put() replaces entire item, update() modifies specific attributes. SkillRegistry uses update() for recordUsage() (increment use_count) and incrementDownloadCount() (increment download_count).

name: skill-ecosystem-three-builder-decomposition
description: Phase 3 skill activation decomposed into 3 parallel builders with non-overlapping file scopes: (1) parser+bridge (parser.ts, skill-bridge.ts, agent.ts), (2) registry+security (registry.ts, validator.ts, trust-engine.ts), (3) MCP+tests (mcp-gateway-client.ts, installer.ts, test files). Key: send specs via mail when Write blocked by hooks, instruction skills inject into prompt while MCP skills create callable tools.

name: load-test-scaling-strategy
description: Scaling load tests from 50 to 1000 concurrent sessions requires: (1) Multiple concurrency distributions (100×10, 200×5, 500×2, 1000×1) to test different patterns, (2) Gradual ramp-up test to identify bottlenecks, (3) Cost validation via sample extrapolation (run 100 clients, estimate 1000-client cost), (4) Skill usage tests at scale, (5) Auto-scaling verification. Each test has SLA expectations: 90% success rate, p99 latency thresholds, error rate <5-20% depending on concurrency.

name: lead-agent-two-builder-parallel
description: For production-hardening tasks with CDK infra + docs/tests scope, split into (1) CDK builder for infra changes (observability-stack.ts), (2) Docs builder for runbooks + DR docs + load tests. Non-overlapping file scope prevents merge conflicts. Both complete in ~10 min.

name: evolution-module-testing-pattern
description: Unit tests for evolution modules use Bun mock() to stub AWS SDK clients (DynamoDBDocumentClient, S3Client, CodeCommitClient, VerifiedPermissionsClient). Pattern: (1) Mock client.send() with @ts-expect-error comment, (2) Return Promise.resolve() with expected shape, (3) Verify behavior via expect() on result values. SafetyHarness methods also mocked for authorization flow testing.

content: AWS SDK evolution dependencies: For evolution modules (auto-skill-gen, iac-modifier, safety-harness), root package.json devDependencies must include: @aws-sdk/client-codecommit, @aws-sdk/client-dynamodb, @aws-sdk/client-s3, @aws-sdk/client-verifiedpermissions, @aws-sdk/lib-dynamodb. These are peerDependencies in packages/core but required as devDependencies for test execution.

agent-self-edit-tools-pattern: Python CodeCommit/CodePipeline @tool wrappers follow same pattern as s3_tools.py/ec2_tools.py - boto3 client inside function, @tool decorator, try/except returning strings. Tools split by service: codecommit_tools.py (5 repo ops), codepipeline_tools.py (7 pipeline ops). All registered in chimera_agent.py load_tenant_tools() function.

name: aws-sdk-v3-type-safety
description: AWS SDK v3 requires explicit enum imports (LanguageCode.EN_US not string 'en-US'). Response properties are optional and need null checks. Array operations (filter/map) on AWS types (Block[]) require explicit type annotations to satisfy TypeScript strict mode. Always import type/enum from SDK rather than using string literals.

name: lead-decomposition-for-new-module
description: When adding a new AWS service integration, decompose into (1) builder-tools: tool files + client-factory + tool-utils + index + peerDeps, following the existing Strands tool pattern from s3-tool.ts, and (2) builder-processor: higher-level module with types, processor class, tests, core index exports, architecture doc. Non-overlapping file scope ensures clean merge. Send full spec via ov mail body since lead hooks prevent file writes.

content: dynamodb-client-proper-types: DynamoDB client interfaces must use proper AWS SDK types instead of 'any'. Import GetCommandInput, GetCommandOutput, PutCommandInput, PutCommandOutput, QueryCommandInput, QueryCommandOutput, etc. from '@aws-sdk/lib-dynamodb'. Maintains type safety for dependency-injected clients.

name: security-fix-decomposition-by-package
description: When fixing cross-cutting security issues across a monorepo, decompose builders by package boundary (core/gateway/infra) rather than by issue type. Each builder handles all CRITICALs for their package. This gives non-overlapping file scope naturally while ensuring complete coverage.

name: aws-tools-infrastructure-preparation
description: Shared infrastructure files (client-factory.ts, tool-utils.ts, index.ts, package.json) prepared before individual tool files. Pattern: (1) Add client imports and AWSClient union type, (2) Add getXClient() method using getOrCreateClient generic, (3) Add SERVICE_RETRYABLE_ERRORS constant, (4) Add commented export for tool factory, (5) Add peerDependency. Enables parallel development of 21 tool files by other builders.

content: tier3-aws-tool-pattern: Tier 3 data/analytics tools (RDS, Redshift, Glue, Athena, OpenSearch) follow same factory pattern as Tier 1 tools. Each tool file exports createXxxTools(clientFactory) returning 6-operation tool array. Standard operations: describe/list (query metadata), create (provision resource), delete (cleanup), start/stop or pause/resume (lifecycle), modify/update (configuration changes). All use Zod input schemas with standard tenantId/agentId/region fields.

aws-tool-error-constants-centralization: All AWS service retryable error constants defined in tool-utils.ts (RDS_RETRYABLE_ERRORS, REDSHIFT_RETRYABLE_ERRORS, etc.), not duplicated in individual tool files. Tool files import constants via 'import { retryWithBackoff, formatToolError, XXX_RETRYABLE_ERRORS } from ./tool-utils'. formatToolError() aggregates all error types for consistent retry detection across services.

name: data-service-pause-resume-pattern
description: RDS and Redshift tools include pause/resume operations for cost optimization (compute charges stop when paused, storage charges continue). Critical for agents managing tenant resources autonomously — allows cost-aware scheduling (pause during off-hours, resume on-demand). Pattern: pauseXxx/resumeXxx tools with single clusterIdentifier/dbInstanceIdentifier input, return current state in response.

content: aws-tool-tag-format-variance: CodeBuild Tag type uses lowercase 'key'/'value' fields, not 'Key'/'Value'. Must transform createResourceTags() output: tags.map(t => ({ key: t.Key, value: t.Value }))

sagemaker-runtime-split: SageMaker InvokeEndpoint requires separate @aws-sdk/client-sagemaker-runtime package, not included in @aws-sdk/client-sagemaker. Model management and endpoint operations in base client, inference in runtime client.

blocked-tool-placeholder-pattern: When infrastructure missing (client factory getters, retryable error constants), create placeholder tool file with: (1) clear error message documenting requirements, (2) copy-paste implementation instructions, (3) single placeholder tool returning error to maintain factory signature.

name: aws-sdk-tool-file-pattern
description: Each AWS tool file follows: (1) factory function createXxxTools(clientFactory) returning Tool[], (2) imports from ./strands-agents (tool), zod (z), ./client-factory (AWSClientFactory, createResourceTags), ./tool-utils (retryWithBackoff, formatToolError, XXX_RETRYABLE_ERRORS), (3) each tool has tenantId/agentId/region in inputSchema, (4) callback: startTime, get client via factory, build Command, retryWithBackoff, return JSON.stringify({success, data, metadata}), catch formatToolError. Average 300-500 lines per service, 4-8 operations per service.

content: test-peer-dependency-modules: When testing modules with AWS SDK peer dependencies (@aws-sdk/*), avoid importing implementation files that load SDK at top level. Instead: (1) Test only type definitions and enums from separate files, or (2) Create mock implementations inline. Example: event-bus.ts imports @aws-sdk/client-eventbridge, so tests import only from event-types.ts.

mock-dynamodb-update-accumulation: DynamoDB update mock must clone items and nested objects to avoid reference issues. Use (item.field || 0) + value to simulate if_not_exists(field, 0) + value behavior for proper accumulation across multiple calls.

cognito-token-validity-units: CDK converts Duration.hours()/days() to minutes internally in CloudFormation template. Test assertions must use minute values: 1 hour = 60 min, 8 hours = 480 min, 30 days = 43200 min.
cognito-auth-flows-allow-prefix: CDK adds ALLOW_ prefix to Cognito ExplicitAuthFlows. Test for ALLOW_USER_SRP_AUTH not USER_SRP_AUTH.

name: cli-deployment-orchestration
description: CLI commands orchestrate AWS deployment: (1) deploy - creates CodeCommit repo, pushes source, runs bootstrap script, saves config, (2) connect - queries CloudFormation outputs, saves API/WebSocket URLs + Cognito IDs to ~/.chimera/config.json, (3) status - displays stack health, pipeline status with table formatting

content: cli-aws-sdk-peerdependencies: CLI package declares @aws-sdk/client-codecommit, @aws-sdk/client-codepipeline, @aws-sdk/client-cloudformation as peerDependencies (not dependencies) following aws-sdk-dependencies-pattern from core packages

cli-deploy-flow-pattern: CLI deploy flow uses execSync for git/CDK operations and AWS SDK v3 for API calls (CodeCommit, CloudFormation). STS auth via aws CLI execSync, CodeCommit repo creation via SDK, CDK deploy via bootstrap.sh with input sanitization (regex whitelist). Config persists DeploymentConfig with account/region/endpoints/status to ~/.chimera/config.json.

lead-agent-hook-constraints: Lead agents in overstory are restricted to read-only file operations plus ov/sd/mulch/git-read commands. Cannot use Edit, Write, Agent, TaskCreate, or file-modifying bash commands. Must delegate all implementation to builder agents via ov sling. Specs must be sent via ov mail (not spec files) due to Write block.

name: bun-single-binary-compilation
description: Use 'bun build src/cli.ts --compile --target=<platform>' to create standalone executables. Targets: bun-linux-x64, bun-linux-arm64, bun-darwin-x64, bun-darwin-arm64, bun-windows-x64. Bundles entire app + dependencies + Bun runtime into one binary. No Node.js/npm installation required for users.

content: cli-bun-compile-scripts-pattern: CLI package.json includes per-target compile scripts (compile:linux-x64, compile:darwin-x64, compile:darwin-arm64) plus compile (native) and compile:all (all targets). Root package.json exposes compile:cli and compile:cli:all. Entry point for compilation is src/cli.ts (not bin/chimera).

name: release-automation-tag-driven
description: scripts/release.ts creates annotated git tags (vX.Y.Z) which trigger .github/workflows/release.yml. Release workflow compiles platform binaries and creates GitHub Release with artifacts. No version bumping in package.json — version is the tag itself.

name: barrel-file-corruption-recovery
description: When TypeScript barrel files (index.ts) get corrupted with AI prose, rebuild by: (1) ls directory to identify all module files, (2) grep exports from each file to find public API, (3) rewrite index.ts with organized export groups (factories, types, utilities, constants). For aws-tools: 19 tool factories, AWSClientFactory, 6 type exports, 2 utility functions, 24 error constant arrays.
barrel-file-ai-prose-corruption: AI model wrote conversational prose into packages/core/src/aws-tools/index.ts instead of TypeScript code, breaking all aws-tools imports across @chimera/core
resolution: Rewrote index.ts with proper re-exports for all 19 tool factories, client-factory, types, and tool-utils. No package.json changes needed (deps already present).

content: lead-agent-hook-constraints: Lead agents in overstory are restricted to read-only file operations plus ov/sd/ml/git-read commands. Cannot git merge, commit, add, rebase, push, or write files. Must delegate all file modifications to builder agents and signal merge_ready for coordinator to handle git operations.

lead-agent-delegation-for-writes: Lead agents in overstory cannot write files (hook blocks Write and git modify commands). For documentation tasks, spawn a builder with file scope and send the full document content as mail body. Builder receives spec via ov mail dispatch, writes the file, commits, and sends worker_done. Lead verifies by reading the builder output file.

name: mock-dynamodb-client-test-pattern
description: In-memory mock DynamoDB client for testing services: Map-based storage, query support for PK match and GSI queries with FilterExpression, attribute_not_exists condition checks. Mock implementation handles: query by PK, query by GSI1-PK, begins_with(SK) filters, tenantId FilterExpression for isolation testing. Pattern: store items as Map with composite key (PK#SK), iterate Map in query() to filter matching items. Enables testing full CRUD lifecycle without real AWS resources.

name: lead-agent-parallel-builder-decomposition
description: 3-builder pattern for auth/integration features: (1) builder-auth-web owns server.ts + auth middleware + login UI + CDK changes, (2) builder-integrations creates standalone route module + admin UI, (3) builder-user-pairing creates core module + updates existing routes. Key insight: assign server.ts to one builder only, have others create standalone modules merged later.

name: tiered-memory-client-pattern
description: TieredMemoryClient manages multiple memory clients (one per scope) with unified API. Constructor initializes SESSION client (always), SWARM client (if swarmId), AGENT client (if agentId). API: storeMessage(message, scope), storeToScopes(message, scopes[]), retrieve(query, scope), retrieveFromScopes(query, scopes[]), clearScope(scope). Enables: write to SESSION for private context, write to SWARM for multi-agent sharing, write to AGENT for persistent learnings, query multiple scopes with merged results.

name: composition-over-extension-memory
description: TieredMemoryClient wraps existing MemoryClient instances (one per scope) rather than modifying the MemoryClient interface. This preserves backward compatibility — existing code using MemoryClient directly is unaffected. New scopes are added by composing InMemoryClient instances with different namespaces.

name: builder-spawn-dispatch-timing
description: When spawning builders via ov sling, the dispatch mail must arrive before the builder reads its overlay and asks for work. Chain the sling and mail send commands with && in a single shell invocation to minimize the race window. If builder asks a question before receiving dispatch, its session may end before the mail arrives. Solution: immediately send a brief dispatch, then follow up with detailed instructions.

name: typescript-discriminated-union-tier-pattern
description: Use discriminated union (ToolTier | 'discovery') instead of expanding numeric enum range. Enables type-safe branching: if (tier === 'discovery') vs numeric comparison. Maintains Record<ToolIdentifier, ToolTier | 'discovery'> for TOOL_TIER_MAP with heterogeneous values.

name: typescript-map-iteration-pattern
description: Use Map.forEach() instead of for-of when building type-safe arrays from Map entries. Pattern: const keys: T[] = []; map.forEach((_value, key) => keys.push(key)); return keys. Avoids Array.from(map.keys()) which loses type inference in some contexts.

name: lead-builder-revision-cycle
description: When builder commits before receiving revision feedback (race condition), spawn a new fix builder with the corrected spec rather than trying to revise a completed builder. The fix builder copies from the original worktree and applies corrections.

content: aws-tool-implementation-pattern: AWS SDK tools follow standardized pattern: (1) Export SERVICENAME_RETRYABLE_ERRORS constant in tool file, (2) Add constant to formatToolError retryable array in tool-utils.ts, (3) Add client getter to AWSClientFactory following getOrCreateClient pattern, (4) Implement tools using tool() function with zod inputSchema, (5) Use retryWithBackoff() for API calls, (6) Return JSON.stringify with success/data/metadata structure, (7) Handle errors with formatToolError(error, region, startTime)

lead-agent-hook-constraints-extended: Lead agents in overstory are restricted to read-only operations. Cannot use Write, Edit, Agent, TaskCreate, or Bash commands that modify files (bun install, git add/commit/merge/rebase). Must delegate ALL implementation to builders via ov sling. Specs sent via ov mail since Write is blocked for spec files.

aws-tools-audit-2026-03-22: 19 tool files exist (Tiers 1-3 complete). 10 target services ALL MISSING tool files: EBS, EFS, Kinesis, API Gateway, EventBridge, SNS, CloudTrail, Config, SSM, X-Ray. EFS partially ready (client factory + retryable errors exist). Config has discovery module wrapper. 7 additional services (DynamoDB, ECS, IAM, CloudFront, Route53, WAFv2) have client factory getters + retryable errors but no tool files.

name: monorepo-peerdep-hoisting
description: Monorepo packages with peerDependencies must have those deps installed in root package.json for tests/typecheck to work. @chimera/core had 40 AWS SDK peerDeps - all needed in root devDependencies.

content: AWS SDK v3: import { WAFV2Client } from '@aws-sdk/client-wafv2' (uppercase V, not WAFv2Client)

quality-gate-deps-root-cause: Quality gate failures traced to empty node_modules + missing AWS SDK peer deps. Core package declares ~35 AWS SDK packages as peerDependencies but root package.json only had ~10 in devDependencies. Fix: add all peer deps to root devDeps, run bun install, build shared package. Discovery modules imported from @strands-agents/sdk which doesn't exist yet — redirected to local strands-agents.ts stub. WAFv2Client vs WAFV2Client naming mismatch in AWS SDK.

name: lead-agent-spec-via-mail
description: When lead agent hooks block Write/Edit, pass full implementation specs to builders via ov mail send --body with complete code snippets, file paths, and line numbers. Builder receives spec as dispatch mail and implements autonomously.

name: client-side-conversation-history
description: Browser-based chat apps store conversation history in localStorage with structure: chimera_conversations (metadata array), chimera_messages_{id} (per-conversation messages). Auto-generate titles from first message. Persist on every send to survive page reloads.

name: web-ui-multi-page-auth-pattern
description: Multi-page web UI architecture with shared auth flow: (1) login.html with Cognito integration, (2) dashboard.html showing metrics/agents/tasks, (3) settings.html for user preferences/API keys, (4) admin.html for tenant management. All pages implement JWT auth check with localStorage token validation and redirect to login on expiry.

content: web-ui-plain-html-pattern: Chimera web UI uses plain HTML+CSS+JS (no framework). Per-page inline styles for page-specific CSS, shared styles.css for base/responsive. Auth via window.ChimeraAuth global (IIFE pattern). XSS prevention: createElement+textContent only. Pages: login, chat, dashboard, settings, admin.

name: frontend-backend-api-contract
description: Frontend auth.js library expects POST /auth/register (name, email, password) returning {userSub, confirmationRequired}. Admin UI expects GET /admin/users returning {users: [{email, name, enabled, createdAt}]} and POST /admin/users/:email/{enable,disable}. Frontend can be developed ahead of backend by stubbing API responses.

name: frontend-backend-api-contract
description: Frontend auth.js library expects POST /auth/register (name, email, password) returning {userSub, confirmationRequired}. Admin UI expects GET /admin/users returning {users: [{email, name, enabled, createdAt}]} and POST /admin/users/:email/{enable,disable}. Frontend can be developed ahead of backend by stubbing API responses.

content: frontend-dom-reference-consistency: Functions that capture DOM element references in try blocks must reuse those same references in catch blocks, not re-query via document.getElementById

name: frontend-merge-conflict-resolution
description: When merging parallel frontend implementations with conflicts in auth.js/login.html, use git checkout --theirs to accept incoming registration feature if it's more complete than HEAD. Verify merged files have required functionality (register() function, registration form UI) before committing.

name: codecommit-api-push-pattern
description: Use CodeCommit CreateCommit API instead of git remote/push to bypass credential helper. Pattern: (1) GetBranchCommand to get parent commit, (2) git ls-files to enumerate tracked files, (3) CreateCommitCommand with putFiles array containing file contents. Eliminates git authentication complexity.

name: lead-agent-builder-dispatch-precision
description: When dispatching builders for search-and-replace tasks across multiple files, provide exhaustive per-file change lists with exact old_string→new_string pairs. Vague instructions ('update all stale values') result in partial completion. Precise instructions ('replace 760 pass with 860 pass in README.md line 160') result in complete work. Include a verification grep command that the builder must run after editing.

content: bootstrap-bunx-cdk-pattern: Bootstrap scripts must use 'bunx cdk' instead of bare 'cdk' command. CDK declared as devDependency, not global install. Pattern applies to all devDep tools: bunx tsc, bunx eslint, bunx prettier. Ensures version consistency across environments.

bun-exclusive-package-manager: Project uses bun exclusively. Use bun/bunx instead of npm/npx for all commands. CDK is a devDependency invoked via bunx cdk, never bare cdk. Documented in CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md.

lead-agent-hook-constraints: Lead agents in overstory are restricted to read-only file operations plus ov/sd/mulch/bun-test/bun-lint/bun-typecheck commands. Cannot git merge/rebase/add/commit or write/edit files. Must spawn builders for any file modifications and signal coordinator for merge operations.
cli-deploy-findprojectroot: CLI deploy uses findProjectRoot() to walk directory tree for package.json instead of git rev-parse. Pure Node.js, no git binary required.
cli-deploy-binary-detection: CLI deploy collectFiles reads content once and detects binary files (null bytes in first 8KB) to skip them. Prevents corruption in CodeCommit commits.
cli-deploy-file-size-limit: CLI deploy skips individual files > 5MB (BATCH_MAX_BYTES) before batching. Respects CodeCommit 6MB per-commit limit at file level.

name: lead-agent-two-builder-revision-pattern
description: When first builder produces mostly-correct work with issues found in lead review, spawn a second builder for targeted revision rather than re-sling the same builder (session already ended). Pass the revision spec via mail with explicit FIX 1/2/3 labels and code snippets for each fix.

content: codecommit-no-changes-batch-handling: CLI deploy wraps each CreateCommit in try/catch to handle 'no changes' errors per batch (error.message contains 'same as' or 'at least one change'). Skip empty batches, preserve parentCommitId for chaining. Avoids forbidden DeleteBranchCommand on default branch.

name: bootstrap-auto-confirm-pattern
description: Bootstrap scripts must accept --yes/-y flag to skip interactive prompts. CLI deploy.ts passes --yes to bootstrap.sh. prompt_continue() checks AUTO_CONFIRM=1 before blocking on stdin. Enables non-interactive CI/CD execution while keeping interactive mode as safe default.

content: bootstrap-yes-flag-non-interactive: bootstrap.sh accepts --yes/-y flag to skip interactive read -p prompts. deploy.ts passes --yes for non-interactive CLI execution. Flag parsed from all args via for loop over $@.

name: cdk-context-key-consistency
description: CDK context keys passed via --context must exactly match app.node.tryGetContext() calls. CLI was passing --context envName= but chimera.ts reads app.node.tryGetContext('environment'). Fix: align context key names

content: cli-pipeline-only-deploy: CLI deploy only deploys Pipeline stack. CodePipeline buildspec deploys all remaining stacks via cdk deploy --all. This is the self-bootstrapping CI/CD pattern.

codepipeline-variable-namespace-pattern: CodePipeline variable references use #{namespace.VAR} syntax with variablesNamespace on CodeBuildAction. Variable names must not contain dots or hyphens. Export via buildspec env.exported-variables.

description: Builders missed items in long multi-point specs. Three builders needed for a 6-point buildspec fix.
resolution: Provide exact file content for simple rewrites, keep mail short to avoid truncation, or split into sequential single-change tasks.

content: lead-agent-hook-constraints: Lead agents cannot Edit/Write files or run git merge/add/commit/push. For even simple single-file changes, lead must spawn a builder. Lead self-verifies via git diff and sends merge_ready to coordinator referencing builder branch.

cli-lifecycle-commands-pattern: CLI lifecycle commands (destroy/cleanup/redeploy) follow existing command pattern: export registerXCommands(program), use ora+chalk for UX, CloudFormation SDK for stack operations, execSync for CDK commands. Shared functions (cleanupFailedStacks) avoid duplication across related commands. Input sanitization with regex prevents command injection in shell interpolation.

name: lead-early-code-review
description: Reviewing builder work in-progress (before worker_done) catches spec deviations early. Reading the builder worktree files during build allows sending revision feedback before commit, saving a full revision cycle.

content: python-agent-uv-pyproject: Python agents in packages/agents/ use uv package manager with pyproject.toml (PEP 621) and .python-version for reproducible builds. Dockerfile uses 'uv sync --frozen' for locked dependency installation. Replaces pip/requirements.txt pattern.

prettier-monorepo-config: Prettier configured at monorepo root with .prettierrc (100 char width, single quotes, trailing commas, LF) and .prettierignore (excludes dist/, node_modules/, bun.lockb, .overstory/, .mulch/, .seeds/, .canopy/). Root and sub-packages have 'format' script. Use 'bun run format' to format, 'bun run format:check' for CI.

monorepo-dependency-alignment: All packages must align devDependencies versions with root package.json. Use exact version (no caret) for eslint. TypeScript-ESLint v8 has stricter rules (no-explicit-any, unused-vars with _ prefix) that surface as warnings in existing code.

token-estimation-budget-gate: Pre-flight budget enforcement uses estimateMessageTokens() with Math.ceil(text.length/4) heuristic. BudgetMonitor returns 'allow'|'warn'|'block', agent throws BudgetExceededError when blocked

bedrock-model-routing-passthrough: BedrockModel.converse() accepts optional turn.modelId to override static config modelId. Agent passes ModelRouter.selectModel() result through to converse call. Budget gate uses estimateTokenCount(text.length/4 heuristic) + BudgetMonitor.getBudgetAction() to block/warn/allow before Bedrock API calls.

python-strands-tool-pattern: Python Strands @tool wrappers use flat functions with @tool decorator, boto3 client per-call, str return type, try/except error handling. Pattern: import boto3 + from strands.tools import tool, @tool def name(params, region='us-east-1') -> str. Each service gets its own module in packages/agents/tools/. chimera_agent.py imports and registers them in load_tenant_tools().

name: builder-wiring-import-verification
description: When a wiring/integration builder merges branches from multiple builders and edits imports, the import names may NOT match actual function names in tool files. ALWAYS verify imports against actual function definitions (grep '^def ' on all tool files) before committing. The import-fix pattern (spawn correction builder) costs an extra agent cycle.

content: aws-sdk-module-level-singletons: AWS SDK v3 clients should be module-level singletons, not per-request instances. Pattern: const client = new XClient({}); at module level, reuse across class instances. For region-specific clients, use Map<region, Client> cache. Benefits: connection pool reuse, reduced memory, avoid credential resolution overhead. Critical for per-request classes like CognitoIdentityProviderClient in Express routes.

uv-lock-dockerfile-requirement: Python Dockerfiles using 'uv sync --frozen' require uv.lock file in repo. Generate via 'uv lock' and commit alongside pyproject.toml. Missing uv.lock causes COPY command to fail during Docker build.
uv-dependency-groups-migration: Replace deprecated [tool.uv] dev-dependencies with [dependency-groups] dev (aligns with PEP 735). Required for uv 0.5.0+.

skill-scanner-pattern: All skill security pipeline scanners follow consistent pattern: (1) Config interface with optional params and defaults, (2) Result interface with 'passed' boolean + findings/violations array + metadata (scannedAt, scannerVersion), (3) Constructor takes optional config, (4) Main async scan method returns Promise<Result>, (5) Private helper methods, (6) Comprehensive test coverage (happy/error/config/edge cases)

name: package-json-merge-conflict-resolution
description: When resolving package.json devDependencies conflicts, prefer newer semantic versions for tooling (@typescript-eslint, eslint) while preserving necessary dependencies (aws-cdk) from both sides. Remove all conflict markers and maintain alphabetical ordering.
builder-stalled-on-large-mail-dispatch: Builder agent builder-write-research stalled for 8+ minutes after receiving large document content via ov mail dispatch. Never wrote file or sent status.
resolution: For document-writing tasks, keep dispatch mail short with pointers to spec files rather than embedding full content inline. Alternatively, write spec file first (if lead has Write access) and reference it. When builder stalls, spawn replacement early (3-4 min threshold, not 7+).

content: orchestration-module-exports: New orchestration modules export via index.ts with pattern: class, factory function, builder, types. GroupChat exports: GroupChat, createGroupChat, GroupChatMessageBuilder, plus 4 type exports. Tests in tests/unit/orchestration/ with comprehensive coverage (lifecycle, message builders, metrics).

github-actions-agent-archive-packaging: GitHub release workflow for agent deployment archives must include bun.lock (not bun.lockb), CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and exclude .claude/.mulch/.seeds directories

gh-release-agent-archive-workflow: GH Actions release workflow produces chimera-agent-vX.Y.Z.tar.gz alongside CLI binaries. New build-agent-archive job runs in parallel with build-binaries. Archive includes packages/, infra/, scripts/, docs/, buildspec.yml, SOUL.md, AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, package.json, bun.lock, tsconfig.json. Excludes node_modules, .git, .overstory, dist, .mulch, .seeds, .claude.

name: cli-deploy-github-release-source
description: CLI deploy supports --source github to download and deploy from GitHub release tarballs without requiring local clone. Uses GitHub API to resolve latest/tagged releases, downloads tarball, extracts to /tmp with tar --strip-components=1 to remove GitHub's owner-repo-sha prefix. Enables zero-clone deployments.

content: packages/cli/src/utils/source.ts provides unified interface for source resolution via SourceLocation type. resolveSourcePath() returns filesystem path for both local and github-release sources. cleanupSource() handles temp directory cleanup. Abstracts source location logic from deploy command.

cli-sync-upgrade-commands: CLI sync command performs bidirectional merge (local<->CodeCommit), upgrade command merges GitHub upstream into CodeCommit preserving agent edits. Both use git credential helper, three-way merge, and provide conflict resolution guidance.

cli-sync-upgrade-git-remote-pattern: chimera sync and chimera upgrade use git remotes with codecommit:: URL scheme (codecommit::REGION://REPONAME) and credential.helper configured per-repo. sync adds codecommit remote to local repo for bidirectional merge. upgrade creates temp branch from codecommit/main, merges origin/main into it, then pushes to CodeCommit — preserving agent self-edits as the merge base.

cli-deploy-source-provider-pattern: chimera deploy supports auto/local/github source modes via SourceLocation discriminated union. Auto-detect: findProjectRoot() returns null (not found) triggers GitHub fallback. GitHubSource uses GitHub API releases/latest endpoint with HTTPS redirect following for tarball download, extracting with tar --strip-components=1. Temp dirs cleaned in finally block.

name: lead-hook-mail-body-scanning
description: Overstory lead agent hooks scan ov mail body text for git command patterns. When sending specs via mail that reference git operations, use prose descriptions instead of literal git commands to avoid hook rejections.
scout-dispatch-timing-gap: Scouts spawned before dispatch mail sent asked 'what should I do?' and two (scout-root-docs, scout-research) timed out before receiving instructions.
resolution: Send dispatch mail IMMEDIATELY after ov sling, or spawn scouts sequentially with mail sent right after each spawn.

name: overstory-dispatch-file-content-pattern
description: For documentation tasks, lead agents send complete file content in dispatch mail body marked with ---FILE CONTENT START--- and ---FILE CONTENT END--- delimiters. Builder agents write the file exactly as provided, commit, and send worker_done.

name: express-to-hono-route-conversion
description: Express routes convert to Hono via: (1) import { Hono } from 'hono' instead of Router from express; (2) const router = new Hono() instead of Router(); (3) handler signature (req: Request, res: Response) becomes (c: Context) where c is Hono Context; (4) c.req.param('id') for params (returns string | undefined, use ! for required params); (5) await c.req.json() for body; (6) c.req.query('foo') for query params; (7) return c.json({...}, status) instead of res.status().json(); (8) Middleware context via c.get('tenantContext') with Variables type definition

content: Express→Hono migration requires systematic 5-phase conversion: dependencies, server init, middleware signatures (async with Promise<Response|void>), route handlers (await body parsing), and context API (c.get/set instead of req properties). Async body parsing blocks synchronous helper functions.

AWS CDK commands must use 'npx cdk' not 'bunx cdk' due to module resolution. Bun's module resolution creates different class instances for aws-cdk-lib constructs, breaking instanceof checks and peer dependencies (error: 'TypeError: peer.canInlineRule is not a function'). Solution: Use 'npx cdk' for all CDK operations (synth, deploy, bootstrap), keep bunx for other tools (tsc, eslint). Update cdk.json app field to 'npx ts-node bin/chimera.ts'.

cli-deploy-pipeline-skip: CLI deploy checks if Chimera-{env}-Pipeline CloudFormation stack exists. If exists, skip local CDK (exit 0 after code push) - CodePipeline handles deployment. If not exists, run deployCdkStacks() to bootstrap Pipeline stack. Prevents exit 1 on subsequent deploys.

cdk-transpile-only-synthesis: Use npx ts-node --transpile-only in cdk.json app field for faster CDK synthesis. Type checking is enforced separately via 'bun run typecheck' quality gate. Shebang removal from bin/*.ts when using ts-node execution.

scout-first-lead-pattern: For simple tasks (3 files, well-defined changes), lead can skip scout spawning and read files directly. Send exact change specs via separate short mails to prevent truncation. Builder died on first attempt due to truncated long mail — split specs into per-file messages.

description: mail-truncation-kills-builders: Long mail bodies sent via 'ov mail send --body' get truncated, causing builders to miss critical spec details. First builder died waiting for complete spec.
resolution: Split specs into short per-file messages (one change per mail). Keep each mail under ~500 chars.

content: tsconfig-skipLibCheck-cdk-synth: infra/tsconfig.json needs skipLibCheck: true because ts-node --transpile-only only skips type-checking of your own code, not .d.ts files from imported packages. Without skipLibCheck, CDK synth fails when workspace packages (e.g. packages/core) have type errors in their declarations.

lead-agent-mail-size-limit: Overstory mail bodies have a size limit. When sending large content (e.g., full file contents) to builders via ov mail, the body gets truncated. Instead of sending verbatim file content, send structured section-by-section instructions so the builder can read source files and construct the output. Split into multiple messages if needed.

name: lead-agent-builder-delegation-for-writes
description: Lead agents cannot write files (hook-enforced). For file creation tasks, spawn builder with --skip-task-check, send spec via ov mail (overview dispatch + per-file content mails). Builder reads template from codebase, creates files, commits, sends worker_done. Lead self-verifies by reading builder diff and files. For ADR-like work (5+ markdown files), one builder handles all files since they share the same template pattern.

name: lead-two-builder-split-fix
description: When a builder misses spec items, respawn a fresh builder for remaining fixes rather than trying to nudge the finished one. Each builder branch touches different lines, enabling clean merge. Keep specs short and atomic — one mail per builder.

name: lead-agent-builder-delegation-for-docs
description: Lead agents with read-only constraints must spawn builders even for documentation-only tasks. Send spec content via sequential mail messages (dispatch + content 1/3 + 2/3 + 3/3) to work around mail size limits.

content: cli-destroy-npx-cdk-pattern: CLI destroy and redeploy commands must use 'npx cdk' not 'bunx cdk'. Bun breaks CDK instanceof checks causing TypeError in security group rules. Fixed in destroy.ts lines 217 and 303.

name: cli-data-archive-before-destroy
description: chimera destroy --retain-data uses CloudFormation DescribeStackResources to discover all DynamoDB tables in Chimera-{env}-* stacks, scans each with pagination, writes JSON to ~/.chimera/archives/{env}-{timestamp}/, saves ~/.chimera/last-archive.json for future reseed. Requires @aws-sdk/client-dynamodb peerDependency.

content: cli-destroy-ordered-teardown: destroy command destroys stacks in reverse dependency order (STACK_DESTROY_ORDER constant) with per-stack try/catch; each stack uses 'npx cdk destroy <StackName> --force' with stdio:pipe to suppress noise

cli-destroy-export-reseed: destroy --retain-data supports --export-path for custom archive destination; reseedFromArchive reads manifest.json table list and uses BatchWriteItemCommand in 25-item batches; items stored in DynamoDB wire format (AttributeValue) can be used directly in PutRequest.Item without marshalling

cli-destroy-data-retention: chimera destroy supports --retain-data + --export-path for DynamoDB table export before teardown. chimera redeploy supports --reseed to reimport archived data. Stacks destroyed in reverse dependency order (11 stacks).

cli-deploy-github-release-default: chimera deploy auto mode defaults to github-release (not local detect). resolveGitHubReleaseUrl() finds chimera-agent-*.tar.gz from release.assets[].browser_download_url — not tarball_url. Throws clear error if asset missing from release.

bun-jest-mock-factory-required: Bun test runner requires jest.mock(module, factory) to always include a factory function as the second argument. jest.mock('module') with no factory throws 'mock(module, fn) requires a function'. Also, jest.requireActual() is not available in Bun; use require() inside the factory instead. Use jest.spyOn() instead of jest.mock('fs') to avoid cross-file module mock pollution in Bun's test runner.

cli-sync-upgrade-pure-sdk: sync.ts and upgrade.ts use pure AWS SDK v3 (GetFolder, GetFile, CreateCommit) for all CodeCommit operations. No git-remote-codecommit, no pip, no credential-helper. sync.ts has zero execSync calls. upgrade.ts uses spawnSync with argument arrays (safe) for GitHub git ops only. Shared utilities in packages/cli/src/utils/codecommit.ts (collectFiles, batchFiles, pushToCodeCommit, getFilesFromCodeCommit).

cli-deploy-github-release-agent-archive: chimera deploy --source github downloads chimera-agent-<version>.tar.gz from GH release assets (not raw repo tarball). source.ts finds asset by name pattern from release.assets[]. Agent archive includes only deployable files.

cli-sync-pure-aws-sdk: chimera sync and upgrade use pure AWS SDK v3 (CreateCommit, GetDifferences, GetFile). No pip, no git-remote-codecommit, no credential helper. Shared utilities in packages/cli/src/utils/codecommit.ts.

bun-test-supertest-missing: supertest is not installed in chat-gateway package.json. Tests that need HTTP integration testing must use lazy require() with early-return guards (if (!request) return) instead of top-level import. Top-level import from missing package causes entire test file to fail loading, blocking unrelated unit tests in same file.

cli-toml-config-override-pattern: All CLI commands load workspace config via loadWorkspaceConfig() at action start. CLI flags override TOML values: options.region ?? wsConfig?.aws?.region ?? 'default'. Remove Commander defaults to prevent override bypass. Set AWS_PROFILE before SDK calls.

cli-toml-write-back: Commands that write state use saveWorkspaceConfig({...loadWorkspaceConfig(), section: {...existing, newFields}}). deploy writes [deployment].source_commit + codecommit_commit. connect writes [endpoints] section.
